title: /adm
text: /adm Specify the directory where the application's configuration files are located. /adm= AdmDirectory Use with All Uniface applications. Description Uniface has multiple adm directories containing configuration files—in common and in the uniface product directory. To ensure that the correct configuration files are used, Uniface applications, including the Uniface IDE, must be started with the /adm switch to set the correct context. /adm should be specified before /asn or /ini Related Links /asn Specify the assignment file used by the application. /ini Specify the initialization file or GUI resource file used by the application.
